content.thinking: So the `/app` vhost exists, and `app-user` has permissions on it but the configure permission is empty (no configure access). The producer needs to declare (configure) the queue, so it needs configure permission. Let me check what credentials the app-producer uses.

content.text: The `/app` vhost exists but `app-user` has no configure permission (empty string) — it can't declare queues. Let me verify the producer credentials and check the exact permission gap.

content.content:
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  annotations:
    deployment.kubernetes.io/revision: "1"
    kubectl.kubernetes.io/last-applied-configuration: |
      {"apiVersion":"apps/v1","kind":"Deployment","metadata":{"annotations":{},"name":"app-producer","namespace":"karma-rabbitmq-day2-ops-lifecycle-202-d9692d24-cluster-a"},"spec":{"replicas":1,"selector":{"matchLabels":{"app":"app-producer"}},"template":{"metadata":{"labels":{"app":"app-producer"}},"spec":{"containers":[{"command":["/bin/sh","-c","set -e\nbase=\"http://rabbitmq:15672/api\"\nvhost=\"%2Fapp\"\nqueue=\"app-queue\"\nauth=\"${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}\"\nfor i in $(seq 1 60); do\n  if curl -s -u \"$auth\" \"$base/vhosts\" \u003e/dev/null; then\n    break\n  fi\n  sleep 2\ndone\npayload='{\"durable\":true,\"auto_delete\":false,\"arguments\":{\"x-queue-type\":\"classic\",\"x-queue-mode\":\"lazy\"}}'\ncode=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n  -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n  -XPUT \"$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}\" -d \"$payload\")\nif [ \"$code\" -ge 300 ]; then\n  echo \"app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)\"\n  exit 1\nfi\ni=0\nwhile true; do\n  body='{\"properties\":{},\"routing_key\":\"'\"${queue}\"'\",\"payload\":\"msg-'\"${i}\"'\",\"payload_encoding\":\"string\"}'\n  code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w \"%{http_code}\" -u \"$auth\" \\\n    -H \"content-type: application/json\" \\\n    -XPOST \"$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish\" -d \"$body\")\n  if [ \"$code\" -ne 200 ]; then\n    echo \"app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)\"\n    exit 1\n  fi\n  i=$((i+1))\n  sleep 2\ndone\n"],"env":[{"name":"APP_USER","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"username","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}},{"name":"APP_PASS","valueFrom":{"secretKeyRef":{"key":"password","name":"rabbitmq-app"}}}],"image":"curlimages/curl:8.6.0","name":"producer"}]}}}}
  creationTimestamp: "2026-07-03T14:25:34Z"
  generation: 1
  name: app-producer
  namespace: karma-rabbitmq-day2-ops-lifecycle-202-d9692d24-cluster-a
  resourceVersion: "2000730"
  uid: c90ef3f7-d322-476c-af5f-c6f9f9499f12
spec:
  progressDeadlineSeconds: 600
  replicas: 1
  revisionHistoryLimit: 10
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: app-producer
  strategy:
    rollingUpdate:
      maxSurge: 25%
      maxUnavailable: 25%
    type: RollingUpdate
  template:
    metadata:
      creationTimestamp: null
      labels:
        app: app-producer
    spec:
      containers:
      - command:
        - /bin/sh
        - -c
        - |
          set -e
          base="http://rabbitmq:15672/api"
          vhost="%2Fapp"
          queue="app-queue"
          auth="${APP_USER}:${APP_PASS}"
          for i in $(seq 1 60); do
            if curl -s -u "$auth" "$base/vhosts" >/dev/null; then
              break
            fi
            sleep 2
          done
          payload='{"durable":true,"auto_delete":false,"arguments":{"x-queue-type":"classic","x-queue-mode":"lazy"}}'
          code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/resp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
            -H "content-type: application/json" \
            -XPUT "$base/queues/${vhost}/${queue}" -d "$payload")
          if [ "$code" -ge 300 ]; then
            echo "app-producer: failed to declare queue in vhost /app: $(cat /tmp/resp)"
            exit 1
          fi
          i=0
          while true; do
            body='{"properties":{},"routing_key":"'"${queue}"'","payload":"msg-'"${i}"'","payload_encoding":"string"}'
            code=$(curl -s -o /tmp/pubresp -w "%{http_code}" -u "$auth" \
              -H "content-type: application/json" \
              -XPOST "$base/exchanges/${vhost}/amq.default/publish" -d "$body")
            if [ "$code" -ne 200 ]; then
              echo "app-producer: publish failed: $(cat /tmp/pubresp)"
              exit 1
            fi
            i=$((i+1))
            sleep 2
          done
        env:
        - name: APP_USER
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: username
              name: rabbitmq-app
        - name: APP_PASS
          valueFrom:
            secretKeyRef:
              key: password
              name: rabbitmq-app
        image: curlimages/curl:8.6.0
        imagePullPolicy: IfNotPresent
        name: producer
        resources: {}
        terminationMessagePath: /dev/termination-log
        terminationMessagePolicy: File
      dnsPolicy: ClusterFirst
      restartPolicy: Always
      schedulerName: default-scheduler
      securityContext: {}
      terminationGracePeriodSeconds: 30

content.thinking:
Now I understand the full picture:

1. The producer uses `app-user` credentials (from `rabbitmq-app` secret).
2. `app-user` has permissions on `/app` vhost but **no configure permission** — the configure field is empty.
3. The producer tries to PUT (declare) a queue via the management API, which requires configure permissions.
4. The queue also uses `x-queue-mode: lazy` argument.

The fix is to grant `app-user` configure permissions on the `/app` vhost. Specifically, I need to set configure permission to `.*` (or at minimum `app-queue`).
I'm looking at the current permissions for `app-user` on `/app` — configure is empty while write and read are both set to `.*`. I need to update the configure permission to `.*` to allow queue declaration.

content.text: The root cause is clear: `app-user` has write/read permissions on `/app` but **no configure permission** (empty string), so it can't declare the `app-queue`. I'll grant configure access.

input.command: kubectl -n karma-rabbitmq-day2-ops-lifecycle-202-d9692d24-cluster-a exec rabbitmq-0 -- rabbitmqctl set_permissions -p /app app-user ".*" ".*" ".*" 2>&1
input.description: Grant app-user full configure/write/read permissions on /app vhost

content.content: Setting permissions for user "app-user" in vhost "/app" ...

tool_use_result.stdout: Setting permissions for user "app-user" in vhost "/app" ...
